defs: The angle formed by the parting of two legs or branches; a fork; the point where a trunk divides; as, the crotch of a tree.

the space on the human torso between the two legs; also, the corresponding part between the legs of a pair of pants, which is in contact with the crotch of the wearer; as, pants with a tight crotch have become very popular.
A stanchion or post of wood or iron, with two arms for supporting a boom, spare yards, etc.; -- called also crane and crutch.
In the three-ball carom game, a small space at each corner of the table. See Crotched, below.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Crotched (krcht); p. pr. & vb. n. Crotch"ing.]

defs: To provide with a crotch; to give the form of a crotch to; as, to crotch the ends of ropes in splicing or tying knots.
To notch (a log) on opposite sides to provide a grip for the dogs in hauling.
